Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Goal Create a Python dictionary representing a Log Skeleton process model Context An order fulfillment process in a distribution center Activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP Structure Keys must be equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Process Flow Logic derivation Standard Happy Path RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D Then billing happens IC to RP Dependencies RO is the start CA needs to happen before RI PI requires RI QC requires PI PO requires QC GSL requires PO D requires GSL RP requires IC

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: IC usually happens after dispatch or in parallel The prompt does not specify exact timing for billing relative to shipping Let us assume it happens after goods are dealt with A common pattern is Invoice after Dispatch

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let us assume the standard happy path where everything happens exactly once for a fulfilled order Let us decide All activities occur exactly once for a complete successful case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously Pair A B means B is before A Flow RI needs CA PI needs RI QC needs PI PO needs QC GSL needs PO D needs GSL RP needs IC CA needs RO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After This is the inverse of Always Before RO implies CA CA implies RI RI implies PI PI implies QC QC implies PO PO implies GSL GSL implies D D implies IC IC implies RP

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Never Together This usually applies to XOR splits In this scenario there are no obvious mutually exclusive activities defined Since no alternative paths are described this set might be empty

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Directly Follows defines the tight sequence RO to CA CA to RI RI to PI PI to QC QC to PO PO to GSL GSL to D D to IC IC to RP Given the series of activities wording I will chain them linearly for the sake of the example model

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Equivalence If all are one then A B is valid for any A B I will select a subset of logical pairs to populate the field meaningfully rather than every permutation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction on Always Before Tuple First Second means If First happens Second happened before Example If RI happens CA happened before Tuple RI CA Correct Self Correction on Always After Tuple First Second means If First happens Second happens later Example If RO happens CA happens later Tuple RO CA Correct

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Critique of Directly Follows Including IC RP implies immediate payment recording In reality this is a wait In process mining exercises unless specified as parallel or async a linear sequence is often the default assumption for the happy path

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Since Cancel Order is not listed and the scenario describes the fulfillment process the happy path therefore one is appropriate for all activities in scope Final check on the dictionary structure Keys equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows Values sets of tuples except activ freq Looks good
